chunk_summary: Guidelines for using the MCP card to ensure effective clinic-to-ICDS and NRC referral handoffs for feeding and care of children under 2 years, with a focus on anthropometry, feeding prescriptions, and program integration.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the MCP card in clinic-to-ICDS and NRC referral handoffs.
Learn how to document feeding status, anthropometry, and growth classification for children under 2 years.
Identify key referral criteria for children with SAM or feeding failure.
Integrate ICDS and NRC programs into child nutrition and health management.
key_facts: The MCP card should document feeding category (EBF, mixed feeding, or non-breastfed), meal frequency, and diversity status.
Anthropometry must include weight, recumbent length, edema check, and growth classification using WHO z-scores.
For SAM cases, referrals must include danger signs, last intake tolerated, dehydration signs, and expected destination (e.g., NRC).
Caregiver follow-up should include ASHA home visits within 72 hours to check feeding and urine output.
Linkages to ICDS programs should include THR enrollment, IFA schedule, and vitamin A dose updates.
reference_quote: Use the MCP card (or discharge/referral note) to transmit a minimum dataset that allows Anganwadi, ASHA, and facility teams to continue the same plan.
document_key_topics: MCP card documentation for feeding and growth monitoring
Referral protocols for SAM and feeding failure
Integration of ICDS and NRC programs into child health management
